questions.question: What was the full name of the eight-year-old girl in the story?
questions.answer: Her full name was Valliammai, but she was called Valli for short.

questions.question: What was Valli’s favorite pastime?
questions.answer: Her favorite pastime was standing in the front doorway of her house and watching what was happening in the street outside.

questions.question: What was the source of unending joy for Valli?
questions.answer: The sight of the bus that traveled between her village and the nearest town was her greatest joy.

questions.question: How far was the town from Valli’s village?
questions.answer: The town was six miles away from her village.

questions.question: What was the one-way bus fare to the town?
questions.answer: The one-way fare was thirty paise.

questions.question: How long did the bus journey take one way?
questions.answer: The journey took forty-five minutes.

questions.question: How did Valli gather information about the bus journey?
questions.answer: She listened carefully to conversations between her neighbors and people who regularly used the bus, and asked a few discreet questions.

questions.question: When did Valli plan to take the bus to avoid her mother’s notice?
questions.answer: She planned to take the one o'clock afternoon bus, during her mother's daily nap time (from one to four).

questions.question: How did Valli save the money for the fare?
questions.answer: She thriftily saved every stray coin, resisting temptations to buy peppermints, toys, balloons, and even avoiding the merry-go-round at the village fair.

questions.question: What was the color of the new bus Valli boarded?
questions.answer: The bus was painted a gleaming white with some green stripes along the sides.

questions.question: Why did the conductor call Valli 'Madam'?
questions.answer: He was a jolly person and called her 'Madam' jokingly because she behaved in a very self-confident and adult-like manner.

questions.question: Why did Valli stand up on her seat during the journey?
questions.answer: A canvas blind cut off her view of the lower part of the window, so she stood up to see over it.

questions.question: What did Valli see on one side of the road while riding?
questions.answer: She saw the canal, palm trees, grassland, distant mountains, and the blue sky.

questions.question: How did Valli react when the elderly man called her a child?
questions.answer: She was annoyed and haughtily replied that there was nobody there who was a child and that she had paid her thirty paise like everyone else.

questions.question: Why did Valli find the elderly woman 'absolutely repulsive'?
questions.answer: She had big holes in her earlobes, wore ugly earrings, was chewing betel nut, and the juice was threatening to spill over her lips.

questions.question: What funny sight did Valli see that made her laugh heartily?
questions.answer: A young cow with its tail high in the air was running right in the middle of the road, in front of the bus.

questions.question: Why didn't Valli get off the bus when it reached the town?
questions.answer: She only wanted to experience the bus ride and intended to go back to her village on the same bus.

questions.question: What did the conductor offer to buy for Valli in the town?
questions.answer: He offered to buy her a cold drink from a stall.

questions.question: What sad sight did Valli see on her way back?
questions.answer: She saw the same young cow lying dead by the roadside, struck by some fast-moving vehicle.

questions.question: How did the sight of the dead cow affect Valli?
questions.answer: It made her very sad and she no longer wanted to look out of the window; the enthusiasm of her journey was dampened.

questions.question: What time did Valli reach back home?
questions.answer: She reached back home at about three-forty in the afternoon.

questions.question: Who was at Valli’s house when she returned?
questions.answer: Her mother and one of her aunts (the one from South Street) were there.

questions.question: How did the aunt describe Valli?
questions.answer: She called Valli a 'chit of a girl' who put her nose into things that did not concern her, acting like a grown lady.

questions.question: Why did Valli smile to herself at the end of the story?
questions.answer: She smiled because her mother and aunt were talking about things happening outside without their knowledge, while she had just returned from a secret adventure.

questions.question: What is the message regarding the cycle of life and death in the story?
questions.answer: Valli's experience with the cow shows how quickly life can change to death, introducing a child to the harsh realities of the world.
